content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Let us call a set of pairs of integers S={(A1,B1),(A2,B2),(A3,B3),…,(AK,BK)}S = \{(A_1, B_1), (A_2, B_2), (A_3, B_3), \ldots, (A_K, B_K)\}S={(A1​,B1​),(A2​,B2​),(A3​,B3​),…,(AK​,BK​)} good if it satisfies the following conditions:


1≤Ai<Bi≤N1 \le A_i < B_i \le N1≤Ai​<Bi​≤N
For every permutation PPP of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N], it is possible to sort PPP with the usage of the following operation as many times as you want:

- Choose (x,y)∈S(x, y) \in S(x,y)∈S, and then swap PxP_xPx​ and PyP_yPy​.
The cost of a set is defined as ∑i=1K(Ai⊕Bi)\sum_{i = 1}^{K} (A_i \oplus B_i)∑i=1K​(Ai​⊕Bi​), where ⊕\oplus⊕ represents the Bitwise XOR operator.

Find the minimum cost of a good set.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost of a good set.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : S={(1,2)}S = \{(1, 2)\}S={(1,2)} is a good set with cost 333 due to the following reasons:

P=[1,2]P = [1, 2]P=[1,2] is already sorted and needs no operations.
P=[2,1]P = [2, 1]P=[2,1] can be sorted by using (x,y)=(1,2)(x, y) = (1, 2)(x,y)=(1,2).

Test Case 2 : S={(1,3),(2,3)}S = \{(1, 3), (2, 3)\}S={(1,3),(2,3)} is a good set with cost 333.
